definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_math_uint128_Uint128

cap.description:
cap is a hard cap on total supply that can be achieved by minting.
Note that this refers to total_supply.
If None, there is unlimited cap.

definitions.name: cw20_logo_Logo
definitions.description: This is used for uploading logo data, or setting it in InstantiateData

embedded.description: Logo content stored on the blockchain. Enforce maximum size of 5KB on all variants

url.description: A reference to an externally hosted logo. Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL.

definitions.name: cw20_logo_EmbeddedLogo
definitions.description:
This is used to store the logo on the blockchain in an accepted format.
Enforce maximum size of 5KB on all variants.

png.description:
Store the Logo as a PNG file. This will likely only support up to 64x64 or so
within the 5KB limit.

svg.description:
Store the Logo as an SVG file. The content must conform to the spec
at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scalable_Vector_Graphics
(The contract should do some light-weight sanity-check validation)

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_binary_Binary
definitions.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization
with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>.
See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

burn.description: Burn is a base message to destroy tokens forever

burn_from.description: Only with "approval" extension. Destroys tokens forever

decrease_allowance.description:
Only with "approval" extension. Lowers the spender's access of tokens
from the owner's (env.sender) account by amount. If expires is Some(), overwrites current
allowance expiration with this one.

increase_allowance.description:
Only with "approval" extension. Allows spender to access an additional amount tokens
from the owner's (env.sender) account. If expires is Some(), overwrites current allowance
expiration with this one.

mint.description:
Only with the "mintable" extension. If authorized, creates amount new tokens
and adds to the recipient balance.

send.description:
Send is a base message to transfer tokens to a contract and trigger an action
on the receiving contract.

send_from.description:
Only with "approval" extension. Sends amount tokens from owner -> contract
if `env.sender` has sufficient pre-approval.

transfer.description: Transfer is a base message to move tokens to another account without triggering actions

transfer_from.description:
Only with "approval" extension. Transfers amount tokens from owner -> recipient
if `env.sender` has sufficient pre-approval.

update_marketing.description:
Only with the "marketing" extension. If authorized, updates marketing metadata.
Setting None/null for any of these will leave it unchanged.
Setting Some("") will clear this field on the contract storage

description.description: A longer description of the token and it's utility. Designed for tooltips or such

marketing.description: The address (if any) who can update this data structure

project.description: A URL pointing to the project behind this token.

update_minter.description:
Only with the "mintable" extension. The current minter may set
a new minter. Setting the minter to None will remove the
token's minter forever.

upload_logo.description: If set as the "marketing" role on the contract, upload a new URL, SVG, or PNG for the token

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_math_uint128_Uint128
cosmwasm_std_binary_Binary
definitions.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization
with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>.
See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

definitions.name: cw_utils_expiration_Expiration
definitions.description:
Expiration represents a point in time when some event happens.
It can compare with a BlockInfo and will return is_expired() == true
once the condition is hit (and for every block in the future)

never.description: Never will never expire. Used to express the empty variant

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_timestamp_Timestamp
cw20_logo_Logo
definitions.description: This is used for uploading logo data, or setting it in InstantiateData

embedded.description: Logo content stored on the blockchain. Enforce maximum size of 5KB on all variants

url.description: A reference to an externally hosted logo. Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL.

definitions.name: cw20_logo_EmbeddedLogo
definitions.description:
This is used to store the logo on the blockchain in an accepted format.
Enforce maximum size of 5KB on all variants.

png.description:
Store the Logo as a PNG file. This will likely only support up to 64x64 or so
within the 5KB limit.

svg.description:
Store the Logo as an SVG file. The content must conform to the spec
at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scalable_Vector_Graphics
(The contract should do some light-weight sanity-check validation)

all_accounts.description:
Only with "enumerable" extension
Returns all accounts that have balances. Supports pagination.

all_allowances.description:
Only with "enumerable" extension (and "allowances")
Returns all allowances this owner has approved. Supports pagination.

all_spender_allowances.description:
Only with "enumerable" extension (and "allowances")
Returns all allowances this spender has been granted. Supports pagination.

allowance.description:
Only with "allowance" extension.
Returns how much spender can use from owner account, 0 if unset.

balance.description: Returns the current balance of the given address, 0 if unset.

download_logo.description:
Only with "marketing" extension
Downloads the embedded logo data (if stored on chain). Errors if no logo data is stored for this
contract.

marketing_info.description:
Only with "marketing" extension
Returns more metadata on the contract to display in the client:
- description, logo, project url, etc.

minter.description:
Only with "mintable" extension.
Returns who can mint and the hard cap on maximum tokens after minting.

token_info.description: Returns metadata on the contract - name, decimals, supply, etc.

cw20_query_DownloadLogoResponse
definitions.description:
When we download an embedded logo, we get this response type.
We expect a SPA to be able to accept this info and display it.

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_binary_Binary
definitions.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization
with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>.
See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

description.description: A longer description of the token and it's utility. Designed for tooltips or such

logo.description: A link to the logo, or a comment there is an on-chain logo stored

marketing.description: The address (if any) who can update this data structure

project.description: A URL pointing to the project behind this token.

definitions.name: cw20_logo_LogoInfo
definitions.description:
This is used to display logo info, provide a link or inform there is one
that can be downloaded from the blockchain itself

embedded.description: There is an embedded logo on the chain, make another call to download it.

url.description: A reference to an externally hosted logo. Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL.

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_addresses_Addr
definitions.description:
A human readable address.

In Cosmos, this is typically bech32 encoded. But for multi-chain smart contracts no
assumptions should be made other than being UTF-8 encoded and of reasonable length.

This type represents a validated address. It can be created in the following ways
1. Use `Addr::unchecked(input)`
2. Use `let checked: Addr = deps.api.addr_validate(input)?`
3. Use `let checked: Addr = deps.api.addr_humanize(canonical_addr)?`
4. Deserialize from JSON. This must only be done from JSON that was validated before
such as a contract's state. `Addr` must not be used in messages sent by the user
because this would result in unvalidated instances.

This type is immutable. If you really need to mutate it (Really? Are you sure?), create
a mutable copy using `let mut mutable = Addr::to_string()` and operate on that `String`
instance.

cap is a hard cap on total supply that can be achieved by minting.
Note that this refers to total_supply.
If None, there is unlimited cap.
